lines.text: (over the studio feed) [pause] They're telling me we can call it, the whole board flipped in about a second, everything that was red an hour ago is blue clear across the country now.
They are dropping the balloons on us here, folks, [pause] forgive me, they are coming down right on the desk.
(over the studio feed) History is healing. [pause] History is healing.
It's a reconciliation. [pause] With a whole world the last administration spent years insulting, and tonight that world is watching us come back to the table.
(over the arena PA) Please rise as the flags are presented together, [pause] the United Nations beside our own, both of them raised to the very same height.
(over the arena PA) [deliberate] I do not stand here to celebrate a victory. [pause] I stand here to begin a repair.
On behalf of the United States, to every nation we have wronged, [pause] we are sorry.
[deliberate] This is not retreat. This is not surrender. This is repair.
(over the studio feed) And the Nobel Committee just handed him the Peace Prize tonight, and they are citing, [pause] this is their word for it, the announced agenda.
(over the footage) Those are pallets down that whole bay, banded hundreds, and the tag zip-tied to the front one there reads Iran Compensation Framework, [pause] disbursement one of twelve.
(over the footage) And in Brussels tonight the Treasury Secretary is putting his name on the last page of it, [pause] two hundred billion dollars, American money, into European defense renewal.

(over the studio feed) That is the White House unveiling what they are calling the Stewardship Act, private assets over a billion dollars to be administered, [pause] their phrasing, in the public interest.
(over the footage) And that is Magnus Hale of Frontier, [pause] sitting alone at that table with one page in front of him.
(reading) [flatly] Frontier is proud to serve the goals of this administration, at home and beyond it.

[flatly] under the carbon framework the sale, use, and private storage of consumer fireworks is prohibited nationwide effective the first of the month, with the Department directing local surrender of existing —
